item.content:encoded: It might be short but it casts a long shadow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Zapped by a shrink ray, cried for help in the database but fails do to no description and got eaten by rats. Ha, thats a bad way to go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God bless the random button, this is hilarious.

item.title: Great but needs work
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this. It's clever and unique. I think it might need to be a -J or a tale though. It's really good, but I'm not sure it belongs on the main list. If this was an addendum at the end of an actual article on a shrink ray, it might fit better. You could extend the joke by having it be labeled as "unintelligible document recovered from the desk of Researcher XXXX, who is currently missing, and was last seen working on SCP-4780", or something like that.
Still +1'ed anyway

item.title: Re: Bad. Very bad...
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand why that object have <+150

Take a look at some of the positive comments in the discussion thread. Other readers enjoyed the piece for various reasons.

Little jokes in serious articles isn't bad, but that… Where's description? Special containment procedures?

SCP articles are considered a "snapshot in time", so technically if it makes sense from a narrative context to have an article be in-progress or unfinished, an author will keep the piece in that state.

I think you need write article for that beam reducing and write special addition about that incident.

The reason this works for a lot of people is because it doesn't have the usual formatting as nearly every other article on the mainsite. People like to see novelty and unique material at least once in awhile.

Of course, if you don't like the piece that's entirely your prerogative; however, it's good to be mindful of others' opinions when it comes to questioning the rating on a piece.

item.title: Bad. Very bad...
item.content:encoded:
I don't understand why that object have <+150. This is a -J object. Little jokes in serious articles isn't bad, but that… Where's description? Special containment procedures? I think you need write article for that beam reducing and write special addition about that incident.

Minus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right? If anything, articles that ARE very consequential to a story are what should be tales.

item.title: Re: +150
item.content:encoded: Brevity is the soul of wit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is freaking brilliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gee, if only there were some way to LOCK the caps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the stupidest goddamn thing I’ve seen all week.

+1, I love it.
Towering above all others

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does "anomaly is relatively inconsequential in the story" translate to "tale?" I'm not seeing the particular logic there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really cool, but it should be a tale. The anomaly itself is practically inconsequential.

-1 in its current format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending is gold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mark of a good -J is its presence on the mainlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Y'know what, I can't deny that the first line alone caught my attention. The spelling and grammar made it hard to read but it makes sense considering what happened.

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, I think it is really funny if you are not so familiar with the comedy. But considering that most of the readers are not babies, the object shouldn't be on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem with that is… there's nothing interesting about the shrink ray. This means that any detailed description of the shrink ray itself would end up being filler, and detract from the story being told.

I think it's fine as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know really. On one end, it dosen't really interest me much, and as an SCP I have no idea what it is. But this is incredibly creative and written well. I think I will +1, as the story couldn't work under another medium and is written incredibly well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: agreed, this should be a -J, not a mainlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, the SCP itself is a shrink ray, nothing special. However, this document was written by a researcher who got hit by the shrink ray, and this researcher is desperately trying to get attention through the use of making its object class Apollyon and trying to upload it to the database.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it. Can someone explain?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Y'know, after giving it some thought, I honestly agree. I think this would be better if initially the article made no mention of the shrink ray itself (I mean, I imagine his co-researchers would be aware of the object he was writing the official document for and it's basic functions, before it goes up), and instead had a "you are viewing an outdated version, click to see revised entry" -type link at the bottom, similar to what gnome suggested, leading into the actual document for the shrink ray. In my mind, this would allow for a short and strange first impression, which would then be made even funnier in hindsight.

No-vote for me as well, for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: God dammit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm…this makes me so sad. How does this ending so effectively make me sad?

+1 top-notch shortest fiction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why it is not a -J？

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that idea—its current form being an incomplete draft-mode "previous version" that provides the real payload of the entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a very cute and complete story. You do a lot with a little, and I enjoyed reading it.

That said, I do have to agree with a lot of the comments I'm seeing here: It should be a -J, or a tale.

I'm no stickler for how far a format screw can skew, and I don't believe every main-list article needs to be a novel. The premise, however, is just a step too far into outrageous to be believable. Perhaps if it began as a normal article, then there was an addendum relating to the researcher being exposed to the shrink-ray, I could get into it.

As it stands, I'll have to no-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So……why do you have time to press the capital keyboard instead of typing a whole word?
Short author but my average tall

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: pffffffffhdg this is the content I'm here for +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, I tagged it with the human as the scip: (it's a miniature humanoid, a human who is anomalously tiny). The shrink ray project (presumably) has its own SCP entry in-universe, and ultimately the reason this file was made because the dude is now shrunk and it's very, very important to him that he get rescued.

But ultimately I agree with pastarasta1, it's just a way to frame the story. There are definitely people who review newly made SCP files, and they would see what was going on, help him, then -ARC the entry. It makes sense to me in-universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is that a thing that deserves to be downvoted? I thought SCPs were supposed to be stories in a weird format.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic!

As you said, there is an scp object, the shrink ray, and this was a delightful way to talk about it using the basic SCP format. It doesn't matter where it was found, or, really, anything, just that at the time this was written, this is what's going on.

I love it, and kudos to Burter for coming up with a smart way to get our attention. Well, it may not have technically worked, but good thinking on their part nonetheless!

This thing is the perfect length, any longer just makes his immediate worry about his well-being diminish.

A super +1!

Still think the title in the mainlist should just be something like "HEELLLLLP"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it despite the dumb goofiness of it, though I was on the fence to actually give it an upvote.

I was, until I realised the title reference.1

Oh well.
Preservation is a doozy.

+1

Footnotes
1. "My Social a Sudoku" at time of this post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is a SCP though, the shrink ray itself is the SCP being written about, before the research had a Shrink Moment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
fuckkk every part of my body is telling me i shouldnt like this but yet, i just love this so much.

reminds me a lot of cartoons i used to watch when i was young :P

1+ but format screws have been wearing me thin a bit lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh shit, my favourite small object! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is being shrunk by a shrink ray or a shrink ray itself not anomalous?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s here because it’s a format screw using the confines of the SCP format to frame a story — like how most modern SCPs work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… this is in fact just an incident with a researcher, nothing more ? Not a SCP, right ? Why it is here ?

Down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding the opinion that this one doesn't really seem like a mainlist SCP. Downvoted on that merit.

However, I think the idea itself is fun to an extent. That ending is both dark and comedic.

Also,

not apollyon needed to get attn.

I see what you did there. (☉ ͜ʖ☉)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was expecting a -J tag.
This doesn't strike me as a mainlister.
All skips on the mainlist, are actual skips. Either events, places, objects, beings, ideas, whatever.
This is a cry for help.
It's not even anomalous.
Don't get me wrong, I like it, it's funny.
But to me, this is not a mainlist skip.

item.title: Love it. Easy +1.
item.content:encoded:
Although… I agree with Almarduk… is this really a mainlister? I almost want to downvote it because I feel like it doesn't really belong as an actual skip. Perhaps -J? Not sure.

Just my two cents.

item.title: REWRITE
item.content:encoded: Special thanks to aismallard for helping me overhaul the piece with regards to sentence construction, and to Lt Flops and minmin for their general feedback. The piece is now 128 words, not counting the ellipses at the end, and 592 characters. Thus, it just edges out the previous recordholder, SCP-4465, for the title of shortest mainlist SCP wordcount on the site (2521 doesn't count).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It… Just doesn't click. Feels more like a -J than a mainlist article. Still not very funny.

On a side note, isn't this taking some surprise inspiration from Morhadow's earlier deleted article? Might be nice to credit him, don't you think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno, my gut says this should be a tale.

I really don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is silly but it made me smile. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So he just gets fucking eaten by rats? Classic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I appreciate what you're trying to do, I feel like this either should have been longer, or shorter, but not this. You use a large percentage of your words giving details that feel unnecessary to the core conceit of the article. And there are a lot of times where it feels like he's just someone typing in a hurry instead of typing minimally (which is what I would expect from someone in this position). The core joke of the article might've worked with like, a much shorter follow up, but your punchline comes first, and then you spend like a hundred words justifying your punchline without much of a secondary punch (and I'm not really counting the rats line, that felt like just another part of the justification for the joke). Sure, it's super short, but that means that every word counts even more.

-1. It's a funny joke, but like… the joke itself is like 5% of the skip. I would either figure out how to move your punchline to the end, or make the justification feel as punchy as the joke itself.

Ok, I'm a dumbass and misparsed the very last line. I still feel like the 100 words between the start and the end felt unnecessarily ramble-y, and could've either been punchier or at least more streamlined, but you do have a punchline at the end so novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Object Class: Apollyon

Me: Checks history to make sure this was in fact an ARD skip

Not apolyon needed 2 get attn.

Pfft +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Adjustments made per your feedback!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: agreeing with all of this but +1 because i still like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic shit. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno, the premise is interesting, but I think there's a lot of word choice here that don't make sense. Like

please hurry building is filled with rats. hungry rats. VERY HUNGRY.

Why not just "please hurry bldg filld rats"? Wasting time on the emphasis would just serve to waste time, risking that he wouldn't survive. Especially since doing caps is very difficult if you can only press one key at a time.

Those also an element of self-consistency here, since he says "4m" for "form", but later uses full words that could be easily shortened, like "computer" instead of "pc", but for some reason doesn't.

but didnt unplug ray when fixing fraed wires and was shrunk by ray but ray has reverse can unshrink me please hurry not joke

Likewise, this could be greatly shortened as well, "didnt unplug ray when fix and hit please hurry unshrink not joke" or similar.

Of course the issue with shortening like that is the piece gets even shorter than it is, which I think risks putting it into "gimmicky format screw" territory.

As it is now, -1.

After the edits, I'll upvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw, poor guy. Go hang out with some ants, maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is really dumb and i really like it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was a bit unsure about this but that error massage made me say "oh nooo" out loud.

item.title: Putting the flash in flash fiction
item.content:encoded: This is my first site format screw. Special thanks to ch00bakka, Taffeta, and Jekeled for reviewing it.
